sglang.title: Support dynamic version name in sglang's pyproject.toml
sglang.author: guoyuhong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When we are using sglang's main branch to develop, it's hard to tell the installed version of sglang. It's because the wheel name is a fix one, e.g. 0.4.4.post1 for now.
Therefore, we enable the dynamic version for sglang (we do not change sgl_kernel). If the wheel is built from a tag, it

sglang.title: update pyproject
sglang.author: zhyncs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Running Unit Tests.
 Update documentation / docstrings / example tutorials as needed, according to Writing Documentation.
 Provide throughput / latency benchma

sglang.title: Fix EAGLE3 for llama3.3 70b
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The EAGLE3-LLaMA3.3-Instruct-70B draft model has a different hidden size with the target model. It should be handled in fc layer.
Ref: https://github.com/SafeAILab/EAGLE/blob/08b3e5c48bf7f22c26590d9987574bd99fa5a7c6/eagle/model/cnets.py#L518
cc: @chromecast56 @Liyuhui-12 @hongyanz @merrym

For example, suppose we want to run DeepSeek-V3 on 8xH200. Before this change, it takes ~120s to warmup all 8 GPUs, because each GPU warmup are done serially, which is converted into parallel operations.

sglang.author: yuhsuan-t

When optimizing the performance of MoE models, understanding the expert id distribution helps us to identify the performance bottlenecks and come up with a plan to fix performance issues.  Such information can be captured in python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/topk.py.

sglang.title: [Feature] Support "strict" in function calling
sglang.author: DarkSharpness
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

With function calling integrated into sglang in PR #2700, this PR adds support for the "strict" mode in function calling by leveraging the grammar backend. When the grammar backend supports structural_tag, a "strict" function will automatically generate a structural_tag to restrict the m

vllm.title: Fix zmq IPv6 URL format error
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
While reading this code, I noticed that it attempts to handle IPv6
addresses properly, but forgets to enclose them in square brackets when
constructing the zmq tcp:// url. The brackets are necessary to clearly
separate the IPv6 address from the trailing port number.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbr

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix chat template loading
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make AutoProcessor's chat template take precedence over AutoTokenizer
Fix HF tool calling templates failing to be loaded
Remove "unicode_escape` decoding

FIX #14884
FIX #15095
FIX #15125
Thanks @chaunceyjiang who helped with fixing #15095!

vllm.title: [distributed] fix dp group
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: fix dp group broken by #14899

vllm.title: [Core] Integrate `fastsafetensors` loader for loading model weights
vllm.author: manish-sethi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR integrates fastsafetensor library for loading model weights. Fastsafetensor uses GPU direct storage to load the weights directly from storage into GPU memory, making it faster as compared to reading parameters iteratively from file. See https://github.com/foundation-model-stack/fastsafetenso

vllm.title: [Core] Don't force uppercase for VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When using this env var VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=debug would result in an
error. It had to be =DEBUG. This seems unnecessary. This change makes
the code ignore the case of the env var contents by converting it with
upper().
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [DOC] Add Kubernetes deployment guide with CPUs
vllm.author: terrytangyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This adds the guide to deploy vLLM on CPUs as I mentioned in #14084 (comment).

We probably should not require GPUs as a prerequisite at all. I can help add a CPU-only K8s deployment guide as a follow-up PR if that's helpful.

cc @Hanchenli @KuntaiDu @simon-mo

vllm.title: [Doc] Update docs on handling OOM
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Specifically ask users to set --max-num-seqs to avoid OOM in V1
Bring up more options (VLLM_MM_INPUT_CACHE_GIB and VLLM_CPU_KVCACHE_SPACE) to reduce CPU memory consumption.
Reduce VLLM_MM_INPUT_CACHE_GIB default to 4 (previous 8) as users with 32GB RAM may otherwise run out of memory.
Misc: Update E

vllm.title: [V1][Perf] Simpler request output queues
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Queue operations showed up when profiling high qps.
Since we coalesce RequestOutput objects, we don't need to use an actual queue.
This changes to merge the outputs when added rather than when removed.

vllm.title: [BugFix][V1] Quick fix for min_tokens with multiple EOS
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reported by @andylolu2 in slack. This can affect min_tokens functionality for models that define more than one EOS token in their config.
We need to revisit how SamplingParams.__post_init__ is used generally, as well as test coverage for this specific case. This is a quick fix in the meantime for a

vllm.title: [ROCm][Kernel] MoE weights padding
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Optimization ported over from ROCm/vllm.
Applying weight padding for MoE.
The principle and rationale is similar to FP8 padding in #13231 except here it's for the half precision types.
The optimization is more experimental and does not apply to any MoE model, therefore is disabled by default.
Expand

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode] Enable spec decode for top-p & top-k sampling
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is to enable spec decoding for requests with top-p & top-k sampling.
It is implemented using apply_top_k_top_p to mask the logits of the target model.
While this is more expensive than FlashInfer's sorting-free sampling, I think it's good for the first step.

vllm.title: [Minor][Spec Decode] Remove compiled_softmax
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It turns out that torch-compiled softmax does not provide noticeable speedups:
$ python bench/bench_softmax.py 
VOCAB_SIZE: 131072, NUM_REQS: 16
PyTorch softmax: 0.034 ms
Compiled softmax: 0.040 ms

VOCAB_SIZE: 131072, NUM_REQS: 32
PyTorch softmax: 0.037 ms
Compiled softmax: 0.041 ms

VOCAB_SIZE: 13

Tested with Llama 3.1 70B in pp=4 and pp2,tp2 configurations.
The following command can be used for testing:
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1 vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct --disable-log-requests --model-impl transformers --gpu-mem
